_meta.description: Asia Part 1 subset of the retirement city dataset, structured to match the master schema. All scores are 1-10 (10 = best). Monthly costs in USD for a single person, comfortable lifestyle. Covers Southeast Asia, East Asia, and South Asia.
_meta.modeled_on: Lisbon (id 2 in master dataset): a safe, coastal, developed city with strong healthcare, good internet, an established expat community, and retiree-friendly visa access. Chiang Mai and Penang are the closest Asia analogues for overall retirement value.

schema_notes.scores: All *_score fields are 1-10 integers. 10 = best/most favorable for retirees.
schema_notes.monthly_cost: USD, single person, comfortable lifestyle (not budget, not luxury).
schema_notes.visa_ease_score: 10 = easy long-stay visa or retirement visa available. 1 = very restrictive. Thailand, Malaysia (MM2H), Philippines, and Indonesia (KITAS) score well. China and Vietnam score lower for long-term residency. Japan and South Korea are improving but still moderate.
schema_notes.healthcare_score: 10 = world-class private hospitals, low cost. 1 = poor infrastructure.
schema_notes.safety_score: 10 = very safe. 1 = high crime or instability.
schema_notes.climate_score: 10 = year-round pleasant. Subjective; filter by climate_type.
schema_notes.overall_retirement_score: Weighted composite. Recalculate when user adjusts priority weights.
schema_notes.ids: Integer ids here are local to this Asia Part 1 file (1-65). The master table uses UUID primary keys, so renumber or drop on import as needed.

_meta.supabase_table_sql: CREATE TABLE retirement_cities (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), city TEXT NOT NULL, country TEXT NOT NULL, region TEXT NOT NULL, subregion TEXT, latitude FLOAT, longitude FLOAT, monthly_cost_usd INTEGER, lifestyle_label TEXT, description TEXT, tags TEXT[], visa_ease_score INTEGER, healthcare_score INTEGER, safety_score INTEGER, climate_score INTEGER, expat_community_score INTEGER, internet_score INTEGER, overall_retirement_score FLOAT, climate_type TEXT, language TEXT, currency TEXT, time_zone TEXT, popular_neighborhoods TEXT[], visa_notes TEXT, healthcare_notes TEXT, best_for TEXT[], avoid_if TEXT[], expense_shares JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW());

cities.description: Asia's most celebrated retirement destination — a cool-season highland city of ancient temples, excellent hospitals, world-class street food, and the largest expat retiree community in Southeast Asia.
cities.visa_notes: Thailand's Long-Term Resident (LTR) visa and Non-Immigrant O-A (retirement) visa require $27,000 in a Thai bank or $65,000 pension income. Widely used and well-understood. 10-year LTR visa available from 2022.
cities.healthcare_notes: Bumrungrad-affiliated Bangkok Hospital Chiang Mai and Chiang Mai Ram Hospital provide world-class private care at 10-20% of US costs.
best_for: Retirees wanting Asia's most established expat community
Buddhist culture and temple enthusiasts
Digital nomads transitioning to retirement
avoid_if: Those sensitive to smoke season (Feb–April haze can be severe)
Beach lovers (Chiang Mai is inland)

cities.description: The City of Angels — a chaotic, thrilling megacity with the world's best street food, extraordinary temples, sky-train convenience, and world-class private hospitals that draw medical tourists globally.
cities.visa_notes: Thailand LTR and Non-OA retirement visa apply. Bangkok has extensive English-language legal and financial services for expats.
cities.healthcare_notes: Bumrungrad International, Bangkok Hospital, and Samitivej are among Asia's finest private hospitals — fully internationally accredited and English-fluent.
best_for: Urban retirees wanting world-class food, culture, and nightlife
Health-focused retirees wanting Asia's best hospitals
Those wanting maximum conveniences in Southeast Asia
avoid_if: Those sensitive to heat, humidity, and traffic (Bangkok is relentless year-round)
Retirees wanting quiet or small-town life

cities.description: Thailand's beach paradise — dramatic limestone karsts, Andaman Sea diving, excellent international infrastructure, and a massive global expat community across distinct beach zones.
cities.visa_notes: Thailand LTR and Non-OA retirement visa apply. Rawai and Chalong are preferred by long-term retirees over tourist-heavy Patong.
cities.healthcare_notes: Bangkok Hospital Phuket and Mission Hospital provide excellent international-standard private care on the island.
best_for: Beach and diving enthusiasts
Sailing community (Ao Chalong is a major yacht hub)
Those wanting resort-quality beach living at moderate cost
avoid_if: Those wanting to avoid crowds and tourist infrastructure
Budget retirees (Phuket is one of Thailand's pricier locations)

cities.description: Thailand's Gulf island — quieter and more upscale than Phuket, with palm-lined beaches, a growing wellness scene, and a mellower expat community.
cities.visa_notes: Thailand LTR and Non-OA visa apply. Samui has a small airport with direct Bangkok flights and some international routes.
cities.healthcare_notes: Bangkok Hospital Samui provides solid international-standard private care on the island.
best_for: Those wanting a more relaxed island alternative to Phuket
Wellness and yoga retreat enthusiasts
Retirees wanting Gulf coast beaches with less tourist infrastructure
avoid_if: Those needing specialist hospital care (Samui is more limited than Bangkok or Phuket)
Budget retirees (island premium applies)

cities.description: Thailand's original royal beach resort — 3 hours from Bangkok, with a relaxed pace, excellent golf, long beach, and the highest concentration of European retirees outside Chiang Mai.
cities.visa_notes: Thailand LTR and Non-OA visa apply. Hua Hin is perceived as one of Thailand's safest cities — popular with Swedish and other Scandinavian retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Bangkok Hospital Hua Hin and Memorial Hospital provide solid private care; Bangkok (2.5 hrs) for specialists.
best_for: Golfers (7+ courses within easy reach)
Those wanting a relaxed, safe beach town with European retiree community
Couples seeking quieter Thailand vs. Phuket's party scene
avoid_if: Those wanting big-city amenities or nightlife
Beach purists (beaches are not Phuket-quality)

cities.description: A polarising Gulf coast city with a notorious reputation but significant expat retirement infrastructure — excellent hospitals, affordable condos, golf, and Bangkok proximity.
cities.visa_notes: Thailand LTR and Non-OA visa apply. Jomtien and Pratumnak are the more family-friendly residential zones away from Walking Street.
cities.healthcare_notes: Bangkok Hospital Pattaya and Pattaya International Hospital provide excellent care — Bangkok Pattaya Hospital is one of Thailand's largest.
best_for: Budget-conscious retirees wanting low-cost expat infrastructure
Golfers
Those wanting Bangkok proximity in a beach setting
avoid_if: Those with concerns about Pattaya's nightlife reputation and associated environment
Family-oriented retirees sensitive to the city's adult entertainment scene

cities.description: Asia's food capital and Malaysia's cultural gem — UNESCO George Town, extraordinary Peranakan heritage, world-class hawker food, excellent hospitals, and the most popular MM2H retirement destination.
cities.visa_notes: Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) program — one of Asia's best retirement visas. Requires MYR 1.5M liquid assets and MYR 40,000/month income (updated 2023 requirements). Sarawak MM2H is an easier state-level alternative.
cities.healthcare_notes: Gleneagles Penang and Penang Adventist Hospital are internationally accredited; Penang is a major medical tourism hub rivaling Bangkok.
best_for: Foodies (Penang is widely considered Asia's food capital)
Culture and heritage enthusiasts
Those wanting Malaysia's best expat retirement infrastructure
avoid_if: Those sensitive to heat and humidity (year-round tropical)
Retirees put off by the updated MM2H's higher financial requirements

cities.description: A gleaming modern capital with the Petronas Towers, excellent infrastructure, superb shopping malls, affordable luxury living, and English spoken everywhere — all at Southeast Asian prices.
cities.visa_notes: Malaysia MM2H applies. KL is also popular under the DE Rantau digital nomad pass for tech-sector retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Prince Court Medical Centre and Gleneagles KL are world-class; KL's medical facilities rival Singapore at a fraction of the cost.
best_for: Urban retirees wanting Singapore-standard lifestyle at 40% of the cost
English-first retirees in Southeast Asia
Shopping and food mall enthusiasts
avoid_if: Those wanting beach or nature living
Retirees sensitive to heavy tropical rain and heat

cities.description: Singapore's backyard — a rapidly developing Malaysian city connected to the city-state by a causeway, offering Singapore-proximity at a fraction of the cost.
cities.visa_notes: Malaysia MM2H applies. JB is very popular with Singapore PRs and citizens who work in SG but live affordably across the border.
cities.healthcare_notes: Columbia Asia Hospital JB and KPJ Johor Specialist provide solid private care; Singapore's hospitals 30 min away.
best_for: Those wanting Singapore access at Malaysian prices
Retirees with Singapore ties (family, healthcare)
Budget-conscious retirees wanting modern Malaysian city infrastructure
avoid_if: Those sensitive to traffic at the Singapore causeway (notorious congestion)
Retirees wanting a culturally rich or scenic environment

cities.description: Borneo's gateway city — stunning Mount Kinabalu backdrop, world-class diving at Sipadan, dramatic sunsets over the South China Sea, and a warm local culture.
cities.visa_notes: Sarawak MM2H (easier than peninsular MM2H) is available. KK is in Sabah which has its own immigration rules within Malaysia.
cities.healthcare_notes: Queen Elizabeth Hospital II and Gleneagles KK provide adequate private care; KL for specialist needs.
best_for: Nature and wildlife enthusiasts (orangutan, proboscis monkey)
Divers (Sipadan is world's best)
Those wanting Borneo adventure as a retirement backdrop
avoid_if: Those needing advanced specialist healthcare
Retirees wanting urban sophistication

cities.description: Asia's gold standard of livability — impeccable infrastructure, the world's best airport, extraordinary food from hawker centres to Michelin stars, and absolute safety, all at a premium price.
cities.visa_notes: Singapore has no retirement visa. Long-term residence requires Global Investor Program (GIP, SGD 2.5M+ investment), Permanent Residency (competitive), or Employment Pass. Not easy to retire here unless very wealthy or holding PR/citizenship.
cities.healthcare_notes: Singapore General Hospital and Raffles Medical are among Asia's finest. Singapore's healthcare system ranks in the global top 5.
best_for: Ultra-affluent retirees wanting Asia's absolute best in safety and infrastructure
Those with Singaporean PR, citizenship, or family
Financial and business sector retirees with regional ties
avoid_if: Budget or even mid-range retirees (cost is prohibitive)
Those wanting to retire easily without significant investment

cities.description: The cultural heart of Bali — rice terraces, traditional dance, organic cafés, yoga shalas, and a thriving wellness and creative community in the island's artistic highland interior.
cities.visa_notes: Indonesia's Retirement KITAS (Social/Cultural KITAS) requires income of $1,500/month and annual renewal. Also Bali's new Digital Nomad Visa (E33G) for 5-year stays. Foreigners cannot own land freehold.
cities.healthcare_notes: BIMC Hospital Ubud for routine care; Denpasar (45 min) or Sanglah Hospital for serious cases; Bali's medical care is limited compared to Bangkok.
best_for: Wellness and yoga devotees
Artists and creatives
Those wanting Balinese Hindu culture as daily backdrop
avoid_if: Those with significant healthcare needs
Beach lovers (Ubud is inland)
Those wanting nightlife

cities.description: Bali's upscale beach strip — designer boutiques, world-class beach clubs, sunset cocktails, and a sophisticated international dining scene along the Indian Ocean.
cities.visa_notes: Indonesia KITAS retirement visa applies. Seminyak commands higher rents than Ubud or Canggu.
cities.healthcare_notes: BIMC Hospital Kuta (nearby) and SOS International Clinic; Denpasar for more complex care.
best_for: Upscale beach lifestyle devotees
Fashion and boutique shopping enthusiasts
Those wanting Bali's most sophisticated dining and nightlife
avoid_if: Budget retirees (Seminyak is Bali's most expensive zone)
Those wanting cultural or nature immersion (Seminyak is tourist-commercial)

cities.description: Bali's digital nomad and surf capital — rice paddies giving way to artisan coffee shops, surf breaks, and a young international community making it the world's remote-work hotspot.
cities.visa_notes: Indonesia KITAS or E33G Digital Nomad Visa apply. Canggu has rapidly gentrified — rents have tripled since 2019.
cities.healthcare_notes: BIMC Kuta (15 min) and SOS clinics; Denpasar for serious cases.
best_for: Active retirees with a surf or digital lifestyle
Those wanting Bali's most international, English-forward community
Coffee culture and co-working enthusiasts
avoid_if: Those wanting a quiet retirement (Canggu is busy and increasingly traffic-heavy)
Budget retirees (prices have risen sharply)

cities.description: Indonesia's sprawling megacity capital — 30 million people, monumental traffic, extraordinary food diversity, good private hospitals, and a large diplomatic expat community.
cities.visa_notes: Indonesia retirement KITAS requires $1,500/month income. Jakarta has substantial expat infrastructure for corporate and diplomatic retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Siloam International Hospitals and MRCCC Siloam provide world-class private care in Jakarta.
best_for: Business-connected retirees with Jakarta ties
Urban food and cultural explorers
Those comfortable with megacity chaos in exchange for low cost
avoid_if: Traffic-averse retirees (Jakarta's traffic is legendary)
Those wanting any nature or beach proximity

cities.description: Indonesia's second city — less chaotic than Jakarta, with a strong Dutch colonial heritage, excellent Chinese-Indonesian food scene, and a gateway to East Java's volcanoes.
cities.visa_notes: Indonesia retirement KITAS applies. Surabaya has good infrastructure by Indonesian standards but limited expat community.
cities.healthcare_notes: Siloam Hospitals Surabaya and RS Premier Surabaya provide solid private care.
best_for: Those wanting authentic Indonesian city life at low cost
Bromo/Ijen volcano enthusiasts using Surabaya as base
Indonesia food explorers
avoid_if: Those wanting a large expat community
Retirees wanting beach access (Surabaya is a port city with limited beaches)

cities.description: Indonesia's cultural soul — Borobudur and Prambanan temples, Javanese batik and wayang kulit, a vibrant arts university town, and some of the lowest costs for comfortable living in Asia.
cities.visa_notes: Indonesia retirement KITAS applies. Yogyakarta is very affordable and culturally rich but has limited international expat infrastructure.
cities.healthcare_notes: RS Panti Rapih and RS Bethesda provide adequate private care; Surabaya or Bali for more complex needs.
best_for: Javanese culture and arts enthusiasts
Ultra-budget retirees
Those wanting Indonesia's deepest cultural immersion
avoid_if: Those wanting beach living (inland)
Retirees needing a large English-speaking community

cities.description: Bali's quieter, more rugged sister island — Rinjani volcano, pristine Gili Islands, beautiful surf beaches, and a more authentic Sasak Islamic culture at lower prices than Bali.
cities.visa_notes: Indonesia retirement KITAS applies. Lombok is developing rapidly but still behind Bali in infrastructure and international services.
cities.healthcare_notes: Very limited — Mataram hospital for basics; serious cases go to Bali (45 min flight) or Denpasar.
best_for: Those wanting Bali's scenery without the crowds
Surfers and divers
Budget beach retirees wanting lower prices than Bali
avoid_if: Those needing healthcare access
Retirees wanting international amenities or expat community

cities.description: Vietnam's most livable city — a modern coastal city between Hoi An and Hue, with excellent beaches, the Marble Mountains, world-class golf, and the easiest everyday infrastructure in Vietnam.
cities.visa_notes: Vietnam's E-visa is valid 90 days (extendable). No formal retirement visa; long-term residents use business visas, tourist extensions, or border runs. DT (investment) visa used by some.
cities.healthcare_notes: Da Nang Hospital for High-Quality Medical Treatment and Vinmec Da Nang provide solid care; Hanoi or Ho Chi Minh City for complex needs.
best_for: Those wanting Vietnam's best balance of beach, city, and affordability
Golfers (multiple world-class courses)
Hoi An and Hue cultural day-trippers
avoid_if: Those wanting easy long-term visas (Vietnam has no retirement visa)
Anyone sensitive to typhoon season (Oct–Nov)

cities.description: Vietnam's economic powerhouse — an electric blend of French colonial boulevards, war history, extraordinary food, and a high-energy expat scene making it Southeast Asia's most dynamic city.
cities.visa_notes: Vietnam E-visa applies. Thao Dien (District 2) and Phu My Hung (District 7) are the most established expat residential zones.
cities.healthcare_notes: FV Hospital, Vinmec Central Park, and City International Hospital provide international-standard private care.
best_for: Urban expat lifestyle enthusiasts
Vietnamese food and culture devotees
Those wanting Southeast Asia's most dynamic city at low cost
avoid_if: Those sensitive to traffic and noise (HCMC is intense)
Retirees wanting easy long-term visa (Vietnam's policies require active management)

cities.description: Vietnam's ancient capital — a city of lakes, French boulevards, and 1,000-year-old pagodas, with a distinct northern charm and an increasingly sophisticated food and café culture.
cities.visa_notes: Vietnam E-visa applies. Tay Ho (West Lake) is the primary expat residential district, with international schools and dining.
cities.healthcare_notes: Vinmec Times City, Hong Ngoc Hospital, and Hanoi French Hospital provide solid private care.
best_for: History and architecture enthusiasts (1,000+ year old capital)
Those wanting a more authentic, less touristy Vietnam experience than HCMC
Ha Long Bay proximity enthusiasts
avoid_if: Those wanting warm year-round weather (winters are cool and sometimes chilly)
Retirees wanting easy visa processes

cities.description: The lantern city — a UNESCO ancient trading port of perfectly preserved merchant houses, bespoke tailors, and extraordinary food, with beaches just 4km away.
cities.visa_notes: Vietnam E-visa applies. Hoi An has a thriving expat community relative to its small size; many retirees pair it with Da Nang (30 min) for city amenities.
cities.healthcare_notes: Local clinics adequate for routine care; Da Nang (30 min) for hospital-level care.
best_for: Those wanting one of Asia's most charming small towns as a retirement base
Foodies (Hoi An's cuisine rivals anywhere in Vietnam)
Those combining beach access with UNESCO heritage
avoid_if: Those needing regular hospital access (Da Nang trip required)
Retirees wanting urban amenities

cities.description: Vietnam's beach resort city — a long golden beach, excellent diving, Russian-tourist-influenced resort infrastructure, and very low prices.
cities.visa_notes: Vietnam E-visa applies. Nha Trang has a large Russian expat community which shapes the city's character significantly.
cities.healthcare_notes: Vinmec Nha Trang provides solid private care; Ho Chi Minh City for specialist needs.
best_for: Budget beach retirees
Diving and water sports enthusiasts
Those wanting a sun-drenched Vietnamese resort lifestyle at minimal cost
avoid_if: Those wanting a large English-speaking Western expat community (Russian-dominated)
Retirees wanting cultural depth vs. beach resort lifestyle

cities.description: Cambodia's capital on the Mekong — a rapidly developing riverside city with French colonial remnants, extraordinary food, and the most permissive long-stay visa regime in Southeast Asia.
cities.visa_notes: Cambodia has one of Asia's easiest retirement visa options — the Cambodia My Second Home (CMSH) offers 10-year renewable residence. USD is the primary currency; zero capital gains or inheritance tax.
cities.healthcare_notes: Royal Phnom Penh Hospital and Raffles Medical provide adequate care; Bangkok (1 hr flight) for serious needs.
best_for: Those wanting Asia's easiest long-term visa
USD-currency retirees wanting ultra-low costs
Mekong and Khmer history enthusiasts
avoid_if: Those needing quality healthcare locally
Retirees sensitive to heat and humidity (very hot March–May)

cities.description: Gateway to Angkor Wat — a charming colonial-tinged town where Angkor Thom, Bayon, and Ta Prohm are a bicycle ride away, with excellent food and a uniquely gentle expat community.
cities.visa_notes: Cambodia CMSH visa applies. Siem Reap is recovering after COVID's tourism collapse and costs remain low.
cities.healthcare_notes: Royal Angkor International Hospital provides adequate basic care; Phnom Penh (6 hrs) or Bangkok (1 hr flight) for serious cases.
best_for: Archaeology and ancient civilization devotees
Ultra-budget retirees wanting incredible history as a daily backdrop
Those wanting a small, friendly expat town
avoid_if: Those needing reliable healthcare
Retirees wanting urban energy beyond temples and restaurants

cities.description: The world's most laid-back capital — a sleepy Mekong riverside city of French baguettes and Buddhist temples, where time moves slowly and life is extraordinarily peaceful.
cities.visa_notes: Laos offers a 1-year business/education visa extendable; no formal retirement visa but relatively permissive. Frequent border runs to Thailand used by many long-term residents.
cities.healthcare_notes: Alliance International Medical Centre provides basic private care; Udon Thani, Thailand (2 hrs) or Bangkok for anything significant.
best_for: Those wanting the absolute slowest, most peaceful pace in Southeast Asia
Francophone retirees (strong French baguette-café culture)
Those using Thailand as a medical backup
avoid_if: Those needing healthcare access
Retirees wanting urban energy or entertainment

cities.description: The most enchanting small city in Southeast Asia — a UNESCO World Heritage town where saffron-robed monks collect alms at dawn, French villas line the Mekong, and time seems suspended.
cities.visa_notes: Laos visa situation applies. Luang Prabang is tiny; most practical needs require trips to Vientiane or Thailand.
cities.healthcare_notes: Provincial hospital only — extremely limited. Any serious issue requires evacuation to Vientiane or Thailand.
best_for: Those wanting the world's most beautiful, peaceful small-town retirement setting
Buddhist culture and monastery devotees
Those valuing atmosphere over amenities
avoid_if: Anyone with healthcare needs (genuinely limited — plan for evacuation)
Those needing reliable internet or urban services

cities.description: Myanmar's colonial capital — extraordinary Shwedagon Pagoda, faded British architecture, and a city that was rapidly developing before the 2021 military coup disrupted progress. Currently not recommended.
cities.visa_notes: Following the 2021 military coup, Myanmar is under severe travel restrictions, internet shutdowns, and active conflict. Most Western governments advise against all travel. Not suitable for retirement.
cities.healthcare_notes: Healthcare infrastructure severely degraded; no reliable private hospitals for international standards currently.
best_for: Not currently suitable for retirement planning
avoid_if: All retirees — Myanmar's current political and security situation makes long-term foreign residence untenable

cities.description: Myanmar's royal city and cultural capital — ancient palaces, Irrawaddy River, and the country's most traditional Buddhist heritage. Currently not safe for retirement due to the military coup.
cities.visa_notes: Same Myanmar restrictions apply. Mandalay has seen direct conflict near the city — do not consider for retirement at this time.
cities.healthcare_notes: Severely degraded; evacuation to Thailand required for any serious care.
best_for: Not currently suitable for retirement planning
avoid_if: All retirees — active conflict near and within Mandalay makes this extremely dangerous

cities.description: One of Asia's most underrated cities — extraordinary night markets, a superbly efficient MRT, world-class healthcare, hot spring culture, friendly locals, and a fascinating hybrid of Chinese tradition and Taiwanese modernity.
cities.visa_notes: Taiwan's Gold Card (Employment Gold Card) allows 1-3 year stays for qualified professionals and retirees with high income/pension ($6,000+/month). Specific retirement visa less developed; visa-on-arrival 90 days for most nationalities.
cities.healthcare_notes: Taiwan's National Health Insurance is among the world's best value — expats can join after 6 months. National Taiwan University Hospital and Taipei Veterans General Hospital are world-class.
best_for: Healthcare-priority retirees (Taiwan NHI is extraordinary value)
Food lovers (night market culture is exceptional)
Those wanting Asia's most underrated, genuinely safe city
avoid_if: Those concerned about Taiwan Strait geopolitical tensions
Those wanting warm tropical climate (Taipei winters are damp)

cities.description: Taiwan's oldest city and food capital — ancient temple city with the highest density of historic sites in Taiwan, legendary street food, warmer weather than Taipei, and lower costs.
cities.visa_notes: Same Taiwan visa situation as Taipei. Tainan is significantly cheaper for rent and daily costs.
cities.healthcare_notes: National Cheng Kung University Hospital is excellent; Taiwan NHI accessible after 6 months.
best_for: Taiwan history and temple enthusiasts
Foodies (considered Taiwan's finest food city)
Budget Taiwan retirees wanting warmth over Taipei's damp winters
avoid_if: Those wanting a large expat community (small in Tainan)
Those needing English-language services widely available

cities.description: Taiwan's southern port city — a former industrial hub transformed into a vibrant artistic city with a beautiful harbor, Love River night walks, excellent MRT, and Taiwan's best weather.
cities.visa_notes: Taiwan visa situation applies. Kaohsiung has the best year-round weather of Taiwan's major cities.
cities.healthcare_notes: Kaohsiung Medical University Hospital and Chang Gung Memorial Hospital provide excellent care; Taiwan NHI accessible.
best_for: Those wanting Taiwan's best climate without Taipei's costs
Art and harbor city enthusiasts
Those wanting Kenting beach access (1 hr south)
avoid_if: Those wanting a large expat community
Those needing English as a primary service language

cities.description: The world's most extraordinary city — impeccable cleanliness and order, the greatest food scene on earth, cultural depth beyond measure, and a safety record unmatched in any major global city.
cities.visa_notes: Japan's Long-Term Resident or Spouse visa are the main paths; Japan's new Specified Skilled Worker visas don't apply to retirees. A 'Financial Independent' visa category is under development. 180-day visa-free stays available for many nationalities.
cities.healthcare_notes: Tokyo's healthcare system is among the world's finest. Language barrier is the main challenge — international clinics in Minato and Shibuya cater to expats.
best_for: Those wanting the world's safest and most extraordinary major city experience
Foodies (Tokyo has more Michelin stars than any city on earth)
Japan culture devotees
avoid_if: Those needing easy Japanese language-free existence (challenging without Japanese)
Budget retirees (Tokyo is expensive, though less so than New York or London)

cities.description: Japan's kitchen and comic capital — Dotonbori neon, takoyaki, okonomiyaki, a warmer and more outgoing populace than Tokyo, and Kyoto and Nara just 30 minutes away.
cities.visa_notes: Japan visa situation as Tokyo. Osaka is significantly cheaper than Tokyo for rent and daily costs.
cities.healthcare_notes: Osaka University Hospital and multiple international clinics provide excellent care.
best_for: Foodies wanting Japan's best eating city
Those wanting Kyoto/Nara access with livelier city energy
Budget Japan retirees wanting less cost than Tokyo
avoid_if: Those needing extensive English language services
Retirees wanting a quieter pace

cities.description: Japan's ancient imperial capital — 17 UNESCO World Heritage sites, 1,600 Buddhist temples, geisha districts, tea ceremony culture, and autumn momiji colours that arguably exceed anywhere on earth.
cities.visa_notes: Japan visa situation as Tokyo. Kyoto is particularly strict about development to preserve heritage — housing stock is limited.
cities.healthcare_notes: Kyoto University Hospital and Rakuwakai Otowa Hospital provide quality private care.
best_for: Japan history and cultural enthusiasts
Those wanting the world's most concentrated traditional Japanese culture
Zen Buddhism and tea ceremony devotees
avoid_if: Those wanting nightlife or modern urban energy
Retirees sensitive to extreme tourism crowding at peak seasons

cities.description: Japan's most livable city — ranked by Monocle for years, with a compact scale, extraordinary ramen culture, beautiful Ohori Park, beach proximity, and lower costs than Tokyo or Osaka.
cities.visa_notes: Japan visa as Tokyo. Fukuoka's proximity to Korea and China (by ferry) gives it a cosmopolitan edge.
cities.healthcare_notes: Kyushu University Hospital and St. Mary's Hospital provide excellent private care.
best_for: Those wanting Japan's highest quality-of-life at the most manageable scale and cost
Ramen culture devotees
Those wanting Korea/China ferry access from Japan
avoid_if: Those needing the largest selection of English-language expat services
Retirees wanting an established English-speaking community

cities.description: Hokkaido's capital — Japan's best skiing at Niseko (90 min), extraordinary seafood, famous snow festival, cool summers perfect for those escaping Japan's heat, and a very livable grid-plan city.
cities.visa_notes: Japan visa as Tokyo. Sapporo draws many retirees who prefer its cool summers over mainland Japan's oppressive heat.
cities.healthcare_notes: Hokkaido University Hospital is a major medical research and treatment centre.
best_for: Skiers (Niseko proximity)
Those wanting cool Japanese summers
Seafood lovers (Hokkaido's seafood is Japan's finest)
avoid_if: Those wanting a mild year-round climate (winters are very cold)
Retirees wanting an expat community

cities.description: A city of extraordinary resilience — the Peace Memorial Park and Museum are among humanity's most important sites, while today's Hiroshima is a thriving, beautiful city with a warm local character.
cities.visa_notes: Japan visa as Tokyo. Hiroshima's lower profile vs. Kyoto and Tokyo means less tourism pressure.
cities.healthcare_notes: Hiroshima University Hospital provides excellent care.
best_for: History and peace studies enthusiasts
Those wanting a Japanese city with deep meaning and low tourist density
Itsukushima (Miyajima Island) lovers
avoid_if: Those wanting a large expat community
Retirees wanting maximum English-language services

cities.description: A dynamic, ultra-modern Asian capital of K-pop, palaces, and extraordinary food — the world's fastest internet, globally leading plastic surgery clinics, and a city that bridges ancient Korean heritage with hyper-modernity.
cities.visa_notes: South Korea's F-6 (Marriage) and F-2 (Residency) visas are the main long-stay pathways for retirees. Korean Investor visa (D-8) via investment. No dedicated retirement visa yet.
cities.healthcare_notes: Samsung Medical Center, Asan Medical Center, and Severance Hospital are world-class — many offer medical tourism services in English.
best_for: Korean culture, food, and entertainment devotees
Those wanting medical tourism-quality healthcare as daily access
Tech-forward retirees wanting the world's fastest internet
avoid_if: Those needing easy long-term visa (Korea is working on this but still limited)
Retirees sensitive to very cold winters and hot humid summers

cities.description: South Korea's beach city — Haeundae and Gwangalli beaches, incredible seafood at Jagalchi Market, dramatic mountain-and-sea scenery, and lower costs than Seoul.
cities.visa_notes: Korea visa situation as Seoul. Busan is warmer and more coastal than Seoul — growing popularity with retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Pusan National University Hospital and Inje University Busan Paik Hospital provide excellent care.
best_for: Beach and seafood lovers
Those wanting Seoul's quality at lower cost
Film enthusiasts (BIFF annual festival)
avoid_if: Those needing the full scale of Seoul's expat services
Non-Korean speakers wanting full English availability

cities.description: Korea's tropical island escape — Hallasan volcano, haenyeo diving women, black lava coastlines, tangerine groves, and a distinctly different pace from mainland Korean cities.
cities.visa_notes: Jeju has special visa-free entry for 30 days for many nationalities not eligible elsewhere in Korea. Long-stay requires standard Korean visa. Jeju Free International City offers some investor benefits.
cities.healthcare_notes: Jeju National University Hospital provides adequate care; Seoul or Busan by air for specialists.
best_for: Those wanting Korea's most scenic, natural island setting
Active retirees (hiking Hallasan, cycling around the coast)
Those wanting a gentler Korean pace
avoid_if: Those needing specialist healthcare locally
Retirees wanting an international expat community

cities.description: Asia's most dramatic skyline above one of the world's great natural harbours — dim sum culture, hiking trails rising above gleaming towers, and a financial hub with changing political dynamics.
cities.visa_notes: Hong Kong's Quality Migrant Admission Scheme and Investment Migrant Scheme are the main long-stay routes. General visa-free stays of 90 days for many nationalities. Post-2020 political changes have affected expat sentiment.
cities.healthcare_notes: Hong Kong Sanatorium & Hospital and Matilda International Hospital are excellent private facilities.
best_for: Finance and business sector retirees with HK connections
Those valuing dramatic natural beauty (hiking) combined with world-class urban life
Dim sum and Cantonese food devotees
avoid_if: Budget retirees (one of the world's most expensive cities)
Those with concerns about Hong Kong's changing political environment

cities.description: The world's gambling capital and a unique UNESCO-listed Portuguese-Chinese heritage city — egg tarts, fortresses, casinos, and a fascinating fusion culture just 1 hour from Hong Kong.
cities.visa_notes: Macau's investment residency (MOP 10M+) or long-stay via employment. Standard 90-day visa-free for many nationalities.
cities.healthcare_notes: Kiang Wu Hospital and Centro Hospitalar Conde São Januário provide adequate care; Hong Kong for specialist needs.
best_for: Casino and entertainment industry retirees
Portuguese-speaking retirees wanting an Asian base
Those wanting unique Sino-Portuguese cultural fusion
avoid_if: Budget retirees
Those wanting authentic local culture without casino-resort overlay

cities.description: China's global city — the Bund's colonial skyline facing Pudong's futuristic towers, Michelin restaurants, world-class museums, and a massive established expat community.
cities.visa_notes: China has no retirement visa. Long-term residence requires employment, family ties, or investment. VPN required for most Western internet services (Google, Facebook etc. blocked). Political situation warrants awareness.
cities.healthcare_notes: Huashan Hospital International and Parkway Health provide international-standard English-language care.
best_for: Business-connected retirees with China ties
Those wanting the world's fastest-developing metropolis up close
Foodies and culture explorers
avoid_if: Those needing unrestricted internet (VPN required for most Western services)
Retirees wanting easy long-term visa

cities.description: China's imperial capital — the Forbidden City, Great Wall, Temple of Heaven, and some of the world's greatest history, alongside air quality challenges and China's most complex bureaucracy.
cities.visa_notes: China residency situation as Shanghai. Beijing is the diplomatic hub — embassies provide some support for expat retirees with foreign nationality issues.
cities.healthcare_notes: Beijing United Family Hospital and Oasis International Hospital provide top-tier English-language private care.
best_for: China history and culture enthusiasts
Diplomatic community retirees
Those wanting to live within cycling distance of the Forbidden City
avoid_if: Those with respiratory concerns (air quality can be severe)
Retirees wanting easy internet access to Western services

cities.description: China's most livable city and the panda capital of the world — a teahouse culture, fiery Sichuan cuisine, giant panda research base, and the most relaxed pace in China.
cities.visa_notes: China residency situation applies. Chengdu has a growing expat scene and is significantly cheaper than Shanghai or Beijing.
cities.healthcare_notes: Sichuan University West China Hospital and Chengdu WeRun International Medical Centre provide adequate international care.
best_for: Sichuan food devotees
Panda enthusiasts
Those wanting China's most livable, most relaxed major city
avoid_if: Those wanting unrestricted internet
Retirees sensitive to high humidity and overcast skies (Chengdu has very limited sunshine)

cities.description: The landscape that has inspired Chinese ink paintings for 2,000 years — karst limestone peaks rising from the Li River, terraced Longji rice paddies, and Yangshuo's cycling paradise.
cities.visa_notes: China residency applies. Yangshuo (near Guilin) is more popular with expats than Guilin city itself.
cities.healthcare_notes: Guilin People's Hospital; serious cases go to Guangzhou or Chengdu.
best_for: Nature and scenery lovers wanting the world's most iconic landscape as home
Cyclists and kayakers (Yangshuo)
Those wanting small-city China at very low cost
avoid_if: Those needing international medical care
Retirees wanting a significant English-speaking community

cities.description: Marco Polo's 'finest city in the world' — West Lake's UNESCO beauty, Longjing tea gardens, Alibaba's headquarters, and a sophisticated, wealthy Chinese city just 1 hour from Shanghai.
cities.visa_notes: China residency applies. Hangzhou is wealthier and more aesthetically beautiful than most Chinese cities.
cities.healthcare_notes: Zhejiang University First Affiliated Hospital and Hangzhou First People's Hospital provide quality care.
best_for: Those wanting China's most beautiful lake city
Tea culture enthusiasts (Longjing tea birthplace)
Those wanting Shanghai access with more natural beauty
avoid_if: Those needing easy Internet access to Western services
Retirees wanting a large expat community

cities.description: China's garden city on the Taiwan Strait — colonial Gulangyu Island, clean air and sea breezes, excellent seafood, and a pleasant subtropical climate that's rare in Chinese cities.
cities.visa_notes: China residency applies. Xiamen is considered one of China's most pleasant and livable cities.
cities.healthcare_notes: The First Hospital of Xiamen University and Zhongshan Hospital provide quality care.
best_for: Those wanting China's most pleasant coastal climate
Gulangyu colonial architecture enthusiasts
Seafood lovers
avoid_if: Those needing internet freedom
Retirees wanting a large international expat community

cities.description: China's Silicon Valley — a city that didn't exist 40 years ago now hosts global tech giants, the world's fastest urban rail, and a 30-minute train to Hong Kong.
cities.visa_notes: China residency applies. Shenzhen has a special economic zone status and 5-day visa-free entry for many nationalities via Qianhai.
cities.healthcare_notes: Shenzhen University General Hospital and Hong Kong University Shenzhen Hospital provide good private care.
best_for: Tech-sector retirees with China business connections
Those wanting Hong Kong quality at lower cost
Pearl River Delta business ecosystem participants
avoid_if: Those wanting cultural depth and history (Shenzhen is very new)
Retirees needing unrestricted internet

cities.description: The 'City of Pearls' and India's tech capital — Golconda Fort, Charminar, biryani culture, and a rapidly modern HITEC City tech corridor, with excellent private hospitals.
cities.visa_notes: India's OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card gives people of Indian origin permanent visa-free access and most rights. Standard e-visa for non-OCI holders (5-year multiple entry).
cities.healthcare_notes: Apollo Hospitals Hyderabad and Care Hospitals are internationally acclaimed private facilities at very low USD costs.
best_for: Indian diaspora OCI retirees
Tech-sector retirees with Hyderabad connections
Biryani and Nizami culture devotees
avoid_if: Those sensitive to heat (April–June temperatures can reach 42°C)
Non-OCI foreigners wanting an easy long-term visa

cities.description: India's Silicon Valley — a pleasant highland climate (never too hot), extraordinary startup energy, excellent craft beer culture, global tech company headquarters, and India's best café scene.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Standard e-visa for foreigners. Bangalore has the highest concentration of English-speaking multinational staff in India.
cities.healthcare_notes: Manipal Hospital, Fortis Hospital, and Narayana Health are world-class — Bangalore is a major global medical tourism hub.
best_for: Those wanting India's best year-round climate in a major city
Tech-sector retirees with Bangalore connections
Indian diaspora OCI retirees
avoid_if: Those sensitive to traffic (Bangalore's traffic is among India's worst)
Retirees wanting beach or coastal access

cities.description: The gateway to South Indian culture — Carnatic music, Bharatanatyam dance, magnificent Dravidian temples, Marina Beach (the world's second longest), and India's strongest automotive and healthcare industries.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Tamil Nadu is less liberal on alcohol than other Indian states (note for some retirees).
cities.healthcare_notes: Apollo Hospitals Chennai (flagship), MIOT International, and Global Health City are world-class — Chennai is India's medical tourism capital.
best_for: Carnatic music and South Indian classical arts devotees
Healthcare-focused retirees wanting India's premier medical tourism hub
Tamil culture enthusiasts
avoid_if: Those sensitive to intense heat and humidity (Chennai is very hot almost year-round)
Those wanting a vibrant international expat social scene

cities.description: The 'Oxford of the East' — a pleasant highland city with excellent universities, a vibrant cultural scene, strong Marathi heritage, and close to both Mumbai and the Western Ghats.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Pune is a preferred returnee destination for NRIs and OCI holders.
cities.healthcare_notes: Ruby Hall Clinic, Jehangir Hospital, and Sahyadri Hospitals provide excellent private care.
best_for: Indian diaspora OCI retirees
University culture and intellectual lifestyle seekers
Those wanting Mumbai proximity with more manageable scale
avoid_if: Those wanting beach access (inland)
Non-OCI foreign retirees wanting easy long-term visa

cities.description: India's maximum city and financial capital — Bollywood glamour, colonial Victorian architecture, street food from vada pav to Michelin-adjacent restaurants, and an undeniable electric energy.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Mumbai commands India's highest rents — a significant factor for retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Kokilaben Dhirubhai Ambani Hospital, Lilavati Hospital, and Hinduja Hospital are world-class.
best_for: Bollywood and entertainment industry connections
Indian financial and business sector retirees
Those wanting India's most cosmopolitan, international major city
avoid_if: Budget retirees (Mumbai is India's most expensive city)
Those sensitive to monsoon season flooding and intense rain

cities.description: India's beach paradise — Portuguese colonial churches, spice plantations, golden beaches, fresh seafood, and a legendary laid-back culture making it India's most beloved retirement destination.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Many Russian and other foreign long-term visitors use Goa on tourist visas with periodic exits. Non-OCI foreigners cannot stay year-round easily.
cities.healthcare_notes: Goa Medical College and Apollo Clinic Goa provide adequate care; Manipal (2 hrs) for specialist needs.
best_for: Beach lifestyle devotees
Indian diaspora OCI retirees wanting India's most relaxed coastal state
Portuguese heritage and spice culture enthusiasts
avoid_if: Non-OCI foreigners wanting a permanent base (visa challenges)
Those sensitive to monsoon season (June–September beaches are rough)

cities.description: Kerala's cosmopolitan port — Chinese fishing nets, Fort Kochi's colonial quarter, backwater houseboat access, and the most progressive and literate state in India with relatively low costs.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Kerala has India's highest literacy rate and most developed social infrastructure.
cities.healthcare_notes: Amrita Institute of Medical Sciences and Aster Medcity are among India's finest private hospitals.
best_for: Kerala culture, Ayurveda, and backwaters enthusiasts
Indian diaspora OCI retirees wanting India's most literate, pleasant state
Those wanting colonial port-city charm at low cost
avoid_if: Non-OCI foreigners wanting easy year-round residency
Those wanting beach-resort lifestyle (Kochi is a port city)

cities.description: A tiny French colonial enclave on the Coromandel Coast — bougainvillea-draped French Quarter streets, Sri Aurobindo Ashram, good French restaurants, and a unique Franco-Tamil culture.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Auroville (5 km away) is an intentional community with a significant international resident population — unique in India.
cities.healthcare_notes: JIPMER (government, excellent) and Aravind Eye Hospital; Chennai for comprehensive private specialist care.
best_for: Spiritual and Ashram community seekers
Francophone retirees wanting India's most French-influenced city
Budget retirees wanting a unique Franco-Indian cultural experience
avoid_if: Those wanting beach-resort quality beaches (rocky in parts)
Non-OCI foreigners wanting easy long-stay

cities.description: The Pink City of Rajasthan — Amber Fort, Hawa Mahal, City Palace, and bazaars of block-print textiles and gemstones, in India's most regal and photogenic state.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Jaipur is part of the Golden Triangle tourist circuit (Delhi–Agra–Jaipur).
cities.healthcare_notes: Fortis Escorts Hospital and Narayana Multispeciality Hospital provide quality private care.
best_for: Rajasthani culture, art, and heritage enthusiasts
Indian diaspora OCI retirees wanting India's most regal setting
Textile and gem trading culture lovers
avoid_if: Those sensitive to extreme heat (May–June temperatures can reach 45°C)
Retirees wanting coastal access

cities.description: The City of Joy — Rabindranath Tagore's Bengal, Victoria Memorial, hand-pulled rickshaws, mishti doi, and an intellectual café culture unlike any other Indian city.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Kolkata has the lowest property prices of any Indian metro.
cities.healthcare_notes: Medica Superspecialty Hospital, AMRI Hospitals, and Apollo Gleneagles provide excellent private care.
best_for: Bengali culture, literature, and food devotees
Indian diaspora OCI retirees wanting India's deepest intellectual culture
Budget retirees wanting an Indian metro at very low cost
avoid_if: Those sensitive to heat and humidity (oppressive May–June)
Retirees wanting modern city infrastructure

cities.description: India's sprawling capital of 8 dynasties — Mughal mosques, British Lutyens bungalows, Old Delhi's Chandni Chowk, and some of India's finest restaurants and museums.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Delhi NCR (National Capital Region) is the largest metropolitan area in South Asia.
cities.healthcare_notes: AIIMS (All India Institute of Medical Sciences) and Fortis Escorts Heart Institute are world-class institutions.
best_for: Indian history and Mughal heritage devotees
Indian diaspora OCI retirees wanting India's political and cultural capital
Those wanting India's widest selection of cultural attractions
avoid_if: Those with respiratory concerns (Delhi has world's worst air pollution in winter)
Those sensitive to extreme cold winters or extreme hot summers

cities.description: Gandhi's city and a UNESCO World Heritage historic city — the Sabarmati Ashram, extraordinary Gujarati thali cuisine, India's most entrepreneurial business culture, and very low costs.
cities.visa_notes: OCI card for Indian diaspora. Gujarat is a dry state (limited alcohol) — relevant for some retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Sterling Hospital and SAL Hospital provide quality private care.
best_for: Gujarati diaspora OCI retirees
Gandhi heritage and Sabarmati Ashram devotees
Budget retirees wanting India's safest major city
avoid_if: Those expecting nightlife or alcohol availability (dry state)
Retirees wanting a large international expat community

cities.description: Sri Lanka's capital — a charismatic port city of Dutch and British colonial buildings, excellent seafood, a growing hipster café scene, and a gateway island with extraordinary diversity.
cities.visa_notes: Sri Lanka's Retirement Visa requires $2,000/month income and 5-year renewable permit. Post-economic crisis (2022) the country has stabilised considerably.
cities.healthcare_notes: Asiri Central Hospital and Lanka Hospitals provide solid private care; India (Chennai) for complex specialist needs.
best_for: Those using Colombo as a base to explore Sri Lanka's extraordinary diversity
Budget retirees wanting a post-crisis value destination
British colonial architecture enthusiasts
avoid_if: Those needing the very highest standard of private hospitals
Retirees concerned about Sri Lanka's recent economic history

cities.description: A UNESCO Dutch colonial fortress city on Sri Lanka's southern coast — ramparts facing the Indian Ocean, boutique hotels in 400-year-old colonial buildings, world-class surf, and extraordinary turtle beaches.
cities.visa_notes: Sri Lanka Retirement Visa applies. Galle Fort has become one of Asia's most sought-after small colonial towns for boutique hotel conversions and expat homes.
cities.healthcare_notes: Local hospitals very basic — Colombo (2.5 hrs) for hospital-level care.
best_for: Colonial architecture lovers wanting a living UNESCO fortress
Surfers (Ahangama, Midigama world-class breaks)
Those wanting Sri Lanka's most elegant, small-town southern base
avoid_if: Those needing healthcare access
Budget retirees (Galle Fort commands significant premiums)

cities.description: Sri Lanka's highland cultural capital — the sacred Temple of the Tooth Relic, tea plantation scenery, Peradeniya Botanical Gardens, and a cool, pleasant highland climate.
cities.visa_notes: Sri Lanka Retirement Visa applies. Kandy's cool climate (average 20°C) is a major draw over coastal heat.
cities.healthcare_notes: Teaching Hospital Kandy (public) and several private clinics; Colombo for specialist care.
best_for: Buddhist pilgrimage and temple culture devotees
Tea plantation and highland scenery enthusiasts
Budget retirees wanting Sri Lanka's most pleasant year-round climate
avoid_if: Those wanting beach access (highland, 115 km from coast)
Retirees wanting a large international expat community
